mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales of automobiles and housing starts signal a synchronized consumer demand collapse that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where both series decline, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a supply-chain normalization phase indicates robust manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with rising INDPRO during normalization, or Rank IC < 0.01 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative, prompting investors to rotate into equities as a hedge against currency debasement, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with positive PAYEMS growth, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.5%, the economy enters a contractionary phase that triggers immediate sell-offs in equities due to anticipated earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, or signal fails to predict direction with accuracy < 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that constrains supply capacity and raises input costs, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months exhibiting this divergence, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.
